 {"id":"bd-077e","title":"Add close_reason field to CLI schema and documentation","description":"PR #551 persists close_reason, but the CLI documentation may not mention this field as part of the issue schema.

## Current State
- close_reason is now persisted in database
- `bd show --json` will return close_reason in JSON output
- Documentation may not reflect this new field

## What's Missing
- CLI reference documentation for close_reason field
- Schema documentation showing close_reason is a top-level issue field
- Example output showing close_reason in bd show --json
- bd close command documentation should mention close_reason parameter is optional

## Suggested Action
1. Update README.md or CLI reference docs to list close_reason as an issue field
2. Add example to bd close documentation
3. Update any type definitions or schema specs
4. Consider adding close_reason to verbose list output (bd list --verbose)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:28.448654-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:28.448654-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-077e","depends_on_id":"bd-z86n","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:28.449968-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-0a43","title":"Split monolithic sqlite.go into focused files","description":"internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go is 1050 lines containing initialization, 20+ CRUD methods, query building, and schema management.

Split into:
- store.go: Store struct & initialization (150 lines)
- bead_queries.go: Bead CRUD (300 lines)
- work_queries.go: Work queries (200 lines)  
- stats_queries.go: Statistics (150 lines)
- schema.go: Schema & migrations (150 lines)
- helpers.go: Common utilities (100 lines)

Impact: Impossible to understand at a glance; hard to find specific functionality; high cognitive load

Effort: 6-8 hours","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:16.520465-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:51:30.236012-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T16:51:30.236012-08:00","close_reason":"Already completed - sqlite.go split into 30+ focused files"}
 {"id":"bd-0d5p","title":"Fix TestRunSync_Timeout failing on macOS","description":"The hooks timeout test fails because exec.CommandContext doesn't properly terminate child processes of shell scripts on macOS. The test creates a hook that runs 'sleep 60' with a 500ms timeout, but it waits the full 60 seconds.

Options to fix:
- Use SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true} to create process group and kill the group
- Skip test on darwin with build tag
- Use a different approach for timeout testing

Location: internal/hooks/hooks_test.go:220-253","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T20:52:51.771217-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:23:55.678799-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:23:55.678799-08:00","close_reason":"Already fixed in commit 6b0d0901 - uses SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true} and kills process group on timeout"}
-{"id":"bd-0fvq","title":"bd doctor should recommend bd prime migration for existing repos","description":"bd doctor should detect old beads integration patterns and recommend migrating to bd prime approach.

## Current behavior
- bd doctor checks if Claude hooks are installed globally
- Doesn't check project-level integration (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md)
- Doesn't recommend migration for repos using old patterns

## Desired behavior
bd doctor should detect and suggest:

1. **Old slash command pattern detected**
   - Check for /beads:* references in AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md
   - Suggest: These slash commands are deprecated, use bd prime hooks instead
   
2. **No agent documentation**
   - Check if AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md exists
   - Suggest: Run 'bd onboard' or 'bd setup claude' to document workflow
   
3. **Old MCP-only pattern**
   - Check for instructions to use MCP tools but no bd prime hooks
   - Suggest: Add bd prime hooks for better token efficiency

4. **Migration path**
   - Show: 'Run bd setup claude to add SessionStart/PreCompact hooks'
   - Show: 'Update AGENTS.md to reference bd prime instead of slash commands'

## Example output

⚠ Warning: Old beads integration detected in CLAUDE.md
  Found: /beads:* slash command references (deprecated)
  Recommend: Migrate to bd prime hooks for better token efficiency
  Fix: Run 'bd setup claude' and update CLAUDE.md

💡 Tip: bd prime + hooks reduces token usage by 80-99% vs slash commands
  MCP mode: ~50 tokens vs ~10.5k for full MCP scan
  CLI mode: ~1-2k tokens with automatic context recovery

## Benefits
- Helps existing repos adopt new best practices
- Clear migration path for users
- Better token efficiency messaging","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-12T03:20:25.567748-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-12T03:20:25.567748-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-0w5","title":"Fix update-hooks verification in version-bump.yaml","description":"The update-hooks task verification command at version-bump.yaml:358 always succeeds due to '|| echo ...' fallback. Remove the fallback so verification actually fails when hooks aren't installed.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:23:06.55467-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T22:34:07.290409-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T22:34:07.290409-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented all fixes in code review"}
 {"id":"bd-0zp7","title":"Add missing hook calls in mail reply and ack","description":"The mail commands are missing hook calls:

1. runMailReply (mail.go:525-672) creates a message but doesn't call hookRunner.Run(hooks.EventMessage, ...) after creating the reply in direct mode (around line 640)

2. runMailAck (mail.go:432-523) closes messages but doesn't call hookRunner.Run(hooks.EventClose, ...) after closing each message (around line 487 for daemon mode, 493 for direct mode)

This means GGT hooks won't fire for replies or message acknowledgments.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T20:52:53.069412-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:22:59.368024-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:22:59.368024-08:00","close_reason":"Added hookRunner.Run calls for EventMessage in runMailReply and EventClose in runMailAck"}
 {"id":"bd-1slh","title":"Investigate charmbracelet-based TUI for beads","description":"Now that we've merged the create-form command (PR #603) which uses charmbracelet/huh, investigate whether beads should have a more comprehensive TUI.

Considerations:
- Should this be in core or a separate binary (bd-tui)?
- What functionality would benefit from a TUI? (list view, issue details, search, bulk operations)
- Plugin/extension architecture vs build tags vs separate binary
- Dependency cost vs user experience tradeoff
- Target audience: humans who want interactive workflows vs CLI/scripting users

Related: PR #603 added charmbracelet/huh dependency for create-form command.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-17T14:20:51.503563-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T14:20:51.503563-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-28db","title":"Add 'bd status' command for issue database overview","description":"Implement a bd status command that provides a quick snapshot of the issue database state, similar to how git status shows working tree state.

Expected output: Show summary including counts by state (open, in-progress, blocked, closed), recent activity (last 7 days), and quick overview without needing multiple queries.

Example output showing issue counts, recent activity stats, and pointer to bd list for details.

Proposed options: --all (show all issues), --assigned (show issues assigned to current user), --json (JSON format output)

Use cases: Quick project health check, onboarding for new contributors, integration with shell prompts or CI/CD, daily standup reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-02T17:25:59.203549-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:39:18.598035-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:39:18.598035-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented - see cmd/bd/status.go with full feature set"}
 {"id":"bd-2q6d","title":"Beads commands operate on stale database without warning","description":"All beads read operations should validate database is in sync with JSONL before proceeding.

**Current Behavior:**
- Commands can query/read from stale database
- Only mutation operations (like 'bd sync') check if JSONL is newer
- User gets incorrect results without realizing database is out of sync

**Expected Behavior:**
- All beads commands should have pre-flight check for database freshness
- If JSONL is newer than database, refuse to operate with error: "Database out of sync. Run 'bd import' first."
- Same safety check that exists for 'bd sync' should apply to ALL operations

**Impact:**
- Users make decisions based on incomplete/outdated data
- Silent failures lead to confusion (e.g., thinking issues don't exist when they do)
- Similar to running git commands on stale repo without being warned to pull

**Example:**
- Searched for bd-g9eu issue file: not found
- Issue exists in .beads/issues.jsonl (in git)
- Database was stale, but no warning was given
- Led to incorrect conclusion that issue was already closed/deleted","notes":"## Implementation Complete

**Phase 1: Created staleness check (cmd/bd/staleness.go)**
- ensureDatabaseFresh() function checks JSONL mtime vs last_import_time
- Returns error with helpful message when database is stale
- Auto-skips in daemon mode (daemon has auto-import)

**Phase 2: Added to all read commands**
- list, show, ready, status, stale, info, duplicates, validate
- Check runs before database queries in direct mode
- Daemon mode already protected via checkAndAutoImportIfStale()

**Phase 3: Code Review Findings**
See follow-up issues:
- bd-XXXX: Add warning when staleness check errors
- bd-YYYY: Improve CheckStaleness error handling
- bd-ZZZZ: Refactor redundant daemon checks (low priority)

**Testing:**
- Build successful: go build ./cmd/bd
- Binary works: ./bd --version
- Ready for manual testing

**Next Steps:**
1. Test with stale database scenario
2. Implement review improvements
3. Close issue when tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-20T19:33:40.019297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T19:11:12.982639-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:11:12.982639-08:00","close_reason":"Implementation complete and verified. Warning on staleness check errors is in staleness.go:36-38. CheckStaleness properly returns errors for corrupted metadata and stat failures."}

 {"id":"bd-411u","title":"Document BEADS_DIR pattern for multi-agent workspaces (Gas Town)","description":"Gas Town and similar multi-agent systems need to configure separate beads databases per workspace/rig, distinct from any project-level beads.

## Use Case

In Gas Town:
- Each 'rig' (managed project) has multiple agents (polecats, refinery, witness)
- All agents in a rig should share a single beads database at the rig level
- This should be separate from any .beads/ the project itself uses
- The BEADS_DIR env var enables this

## Documentation Needed

1. Add a section to docs explaining BEADS_DIR for multi-agent setups
2. Example: setting BEADS_DIR in agent startup scripts/hooks
3. Clarify interaction with project-level .beads/ (BEADS_DIR takes precedence)

## Current Support

Already implemented in internal/beads/beads.go:FindDatabasePath():
- BEADS_DIR env var is checked first (preferred)
- BEADS_DB env var still supported (deprecated)
- Falls back to .beads/ search in tree

Just needs documentation for the multi-agent workspace pattern.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T22:08:22.158027-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T22:08:22.158027-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-47tn","title":"Add bd daemon --stop-all command to kill all daemon processes","description":"Currently there's no easy way to stop all running bd daemon processes. Users must resort to pkill -f 'bd daemon' or similar shell commands.

Add a --stop-all flag to bd daemon that:
1. Finds all running bd daemon processes (not just the current repo's daemon)
2. Gracefully stops them all
3. Reports how many were stopped

This is useful when:
- Multiple daemons are running and causing race conditions
- User wants a clean slate before running bd sync
- Debugging daemon-related issues","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-13T06:34:45.080633-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T01:14:49.501989-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-14T17:33:03.057089-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-y2v","title":"Refactor duplicate JSONL-from-git parsing code","description":"Both readFirstIssueFromGit() in init.go and importFromGit() in autoimport.go have similar code patterns for:
1. Running git show <ref>:<path>
2. Scanning the output with bufio.Scanner
3. Parsing JSON lines

Could be refactored to share a helper like:
- readJSONLFromGit(gitRef, path string) ([]byte, error)
- Or a streaming version: streamJSONLFromGit(gitRef, path string) (io.Reader, error)

Files:
- cmd/bd/autoimport.go:225-256 (importFromGit)
- cmd/bd/init.go:1212-1243 (readFirstIssueFromGit)

Priority is low since code duplication is minimal and both functions work correctly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-05T14:51:18.41124-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-05T14:51:18.41124-08:00"}

This prevents proper worktree compatibility.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:32.082776345-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:32.082776345-07:00"}
-{"id":"bd-ykd9","title":"Add bd doctor --fix flag to automatically repair issues","description":"Implement a --fix flag for bd doctor that can automatically repair detected issues.

Requirements:
- Add --fix flag to bd doctor command
- Show all fixable issues and prompt for confirmation before applying fixes
- Organize fix implementations under doctor/fix/<type_of_fix>.go
- Each fix type should have its own file (e.g., doctor/fix/hooks.go, doctor/fix/sync.go)
- Display what will be fixed and ask user to confirm (Y/n) before proceeding
- Support fixing issues like:
  - Missing or broken git hooks
  - Sync problems with remote
  - File permission issues
  - Any other auto-repairable issues doctor detects

Implementation notes:
- Maintain separation between detection (existing doctor code) and repair (new fix code)
- Each fix should be idempotent and safe to run multiple times
- Provide clear output about what was fixed

Requirements:

- Organize fix implementations under doctor/fix/<type_of_fix>.go

- Support fixing issues like:
  - Missing or broken git hooks
  - Sync problems with remote
  - File permission issues
  - Any other auto-repairable issues doctor detects

Implementation notes:
- Maintain separation between detection (existing doctor code) and repair (new fix code)

 {"id":"bd-z86n","title":"Code Review: PR #551 - Persist close_reason to issues table","description":"Code review of PR #551 which fixes close_reason persistence bug.

## Summary
The PR correctly fixes a bug where close_reason was only stored in the events table, not in the issues.close_reason column. This caused `bd show --json` to return empty close_reason.

## What Was Fixed
- ✅ CloseIssue now updates both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ ReOpenIssue clears both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ Comprehensive tests added for both storage and CLI layers
- ✅ Clear documentation in queries.go about dual storage strategy

## Quality Assessment
✅ Tests cover both storage layer and CLI JSON output
✅ Handles reopen case (clearing close_reason)
✅ Good comments explaining dual-storage design
✅ No known issues

## Potential Followups
See linked issues for suggestions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:06.887069-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:06.887069-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-z8a6","title":"bd delete --from-file should add deleted issues to deletions manifest","description":"When using bd delete --from-file to bulk delete issues, the deleted issue IDs are not being added to the deletions.jsonl manifest.

This causes those issues to be resurrected during bd sync when git history scanning finds them in old commits.

Expected: All deleted issues should be added to deletions.jsonl so they wont be reimported from git history.

Workaround: Manually add deletion records to deletions.jsonl.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:48:14.099855-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","close_reason":"Design changed: tombstones now replace deletions.jsonl for tracking deleted issues (see bd-3b4).
